name: Jollof Rice
instructions: Blend tomatoes, red bell peppers, and onions into a smooth paste.
Heat vegetable oil in a pot and fry the blended mix for 10–15 minutes.
Add thyme, bay leaves, salt, and seasoning cubes.
Stir in washed rice and add water to cook.
Cover and simmer on low heat until rice is tender and flavorful.

name: Fried Rice
instructions: Parboil rice and set aside.
Fry diced vegetables in vegetable oil for a few minutes.
Add cooked liver or chicken pieces and stir well.
Add rice, soy sauce, and seasoning, then stir-fry until evenly mixed.
Serve with fried plantains or grilled chicken.

name: Coconut Rice
instructions: Extract or use canned coconut milk.
Blend tomatoes, peppers, and onions and fry the sauce in oil.
Add coconut milk, seasoning, and rice, then stir well.
Cook on low heat until rice is soft and infused with coconut flavor.

name: Egusi Soup
instructions: Grind melon seeds into a paste with water.
Fry the paste in palm oil with onions until slightly thick.
Add meat, fish, and seasoning cubes.
Add water and vegetables, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Serve hot with pounded yam, eba, or fufu.

name: Efo Riro
instructions: Blend tomatoes, peppers, and onions into a smooth mix.
Fry in palm oil until thick.
Add smoked fish, meat, and seasoning cubes.
Stir in chopped spinach and cook for 5–7 minutes.
Serve with rice, yam, or plantains.

name: Okra Soup
instructions: Chop okra finely and set aside.
Boil meat and fish with seasoning.
Add palm oil, crayfish, and salt to the pot.
Stir in okra and cook for 5–7 minutes until thick and slimy.
Serve with fufu or garri.

name: Afang Soup
instructions: Grind afang leaves and chop waterleaf.
Cook meat and fish with seasoning until tender.
Add palm oil, then the leaves and a little water.
Simmer for 10 minutes and serve with fufu or semovita.

name: Moi Moi
instructions: Peel and blend beans with peppers and onions.
Add vegetable oil, seasoning, and salt, then mix well.
Pour mixture into bowls or foil containers and add boiled egg slices.
Steam for 45 minutes until firm.
Serve with pap or rice.

name: Akara (Bean Cakes)
instructions: Peel beans and blend with onions and pepper into a thick paste.
Whisk the paste until light and fluffy.
Heat oil and fry spoonfuls of batter until golden brown.
Serve with pap or bread.

name: Yam and Egg Sauce
instructions: Peel and slice yam, then boil with salt until soft.
Chop onions and tomatoes and fry them in oil for 5 minutes.
Add whisked eggs and stir-fry until cooked.
Serve the egg sauce with the boiled yam.

name: Beans Porridge
instructions: Boil beans until soft.
Add palm oil, onions, and pepper.
Stir well and cook for another 10 minutes until thick.
Serve with bread or fried plantains.

name: Pepper Soup
instructions: Wash and cut meat or fish into pieces.
Add water, onions, pepper soup spice, and seasoning cubes.
Boil until tender and flavorful.
Serve hot with yam or rice.

name: Pounded Yam and Egusi Soup
instructions: Boil yam until soft, then pound until smooth.
Prepare egusi soup by frying ground melon seeds in palm oil with onions.
Add meat, fish, vegetables, and seasoning cubes.
Serve hot with pounded yam.

name: Plantain Porridge
instructions: Peel and chop plantains into chunks.
Add to a pot with palm oil, onions, crayfish, and fish.
Season and cook until thick and soft.
Serve warm.

name: Ewa Agoyin
instructions: Boil beans until soft and mash lightly.
Blend dried peppers and onions.
Fry the blend in palm oil until dark and aromatic.
Serve sauce over beans with fried plantain or bread.

name: Yamarita
instructions: Peel and cut yam into slices, then boil until almost soft.
Whisk eggs and season with salt and pepper.
Coat yam slices in flour, dip in eggs, and fry in hot oil until golden brown.
Serve with sauce or ketchup.

name: Nkwobi
instructions: Boil cow foot with salt, pepper, and seasoning until soft.
Dissolve potash in warm water and mix with palm oil until it thickens.
Add cooked meat and stir well.
Garnish with onions and utazi leaves before serving.

name: Ofada Rice and Ayamase Sauce
instructions: Boil ofada rice until soft and set aside.
Blend green peppers and onions.
Bleach palm oil slightly and fry the blended mix with locust beans.
Add assorted meat and seasoning, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Serve with ofada rice.

name: Oha Soup
instructions: Boil cocoyam and blend into a paste.
Cook meat and fish with seasoning until tender.
Add palm oil, cocoyam paste, and allow to thicken.
Add oha leaves and simmer for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Suya
instructions: Cut beef into thin slices and marinate with suya spice, groundnut powder, and salt.
Skewer the meat and grill over open fire or in an oven until brown.
Brush with oil while grilling for moisture.
Serve hot with onions and tomatoes.
